forecasts.reasoning: Al-Sadr has a longstanding habit of withdrawing from and then returning to Iraqi electoral politics. Although his recent renaming of his movement to the 'National Shiite Movement' indicates intentions for future political organization, his very firm and explicit public rejection of the Iraqi President's plea on April 18, 2025, strongly pushes against an imminent reversal. For this question to resolve Yes, Sadr must announce his party is fielding candidates before July 1, 2025 (less than two months from the knowledge cutoff). Even if he decides to engage in the November 2025 elections, sources close to him mention he is considering an 'indirect path' (which suggests backing independents rather than formally fielding party candidates) and he notoriously waits until the last possible minute to make return announcements to maximize his leverage (often in the late summer, like his August 2021 return for the October 2021 elections). Both the timeline and the 'indirect' nature of potential participation heavily suppress the probability of a formal 'Yes' announcement by July 1.
